description: flushCurrentSession at flush-current-session.ts:42 declares previousFrontmatter as an optional parameter ('previousFrontmatter?: Frontmatter | null') instead of required ('previousFrontmatter: Frontmatter | null'). The spec (behavioral-spec.md:43-44) and the pipeline input type (pipeline.ts:49) both define previousFrontmatter as required (non-optional). The optional parameter means any direct caller of flushCurrentSession can omit it entirely without a TypeScript error, silently passing undefined to blurSave. At flush-current-session.ts:64, the nullish coalescing 'previousFrontmatter ?? null' masks the omission by coercing undefined to null. This weakens the contract: if previousFrontmatter carries meaningful data (for TagInventory delta calculation per NoteFileSaved spec), omitting it would produce incorrect NoteFileSaved events. Multiple tests call flushCurrentSession with only 3 arguments (e.g., step2 lines 87, 106, 133, 187, 198) without previousFrontmatter, which compiles successfully because the parameter is optional.
